rationale: The citing paper uses Hoeffler (2003) for a core construct in its setup, explicitly citing it when introducing the RNP versus INP distinction in the new product literature. It also operationally relies on the cited work by adopting Hoeffler's two-item product newness scale in Study 1a (and then carrying that measurement approach across studies). This goes beyond a passing literature-list mention because the cited paper informs both framing and measurement choices in the empirical design.
relation_description: The citing study applies Hoeffler (2003)'s really new product framing and measurement approach to an advertising-creativity context. It uses the cited paper's product-newness conceptualization and scale to build and test its ad type by product newness hypotheses.
